description: General-purpose PII for non-Windows server/application logs: IPv4 (incl. CIDR), IPv6 (all common textual forms), email, MAC, credit-card (Luhn-validated), US SSN, and narrow phone formats. Pair with `windows-log` for Windows hosts. Each detector's full match IS the sensitive token (context excluded with look-around); `safe` (or, for validator-gated detectors, a deliberately-invalid fake) keeps redaction idempotent and lets the residual-PII scanner flag only un-redacted real PII.
notes: IPv4/IPv6 collide with version strings (e.g. 10.0.0.0) and other dotted/colon-grouped tokens; that is acceptable here (this profile is opt-in for general logs). Keep IP redaction OUT of `windows-log`, where dotted-quads are overwhelmingly version numbers.

IPv4 will NOT redact an address preceded by '-'/'_' (the digits are usually part of a larger identifier) or by '~~' (a Windows assembly-identity / version marker like amd64~~10.0.1.2). A single '~' is allowed (it can be a field separator). Two detectors re-allow a leading '-' under a high-confidence signal: `ipv4-range-tail` rescues the SECOND operand of a glued range 'A-B' (the dash must be preceded by a dotted-quad, so 10.0.0.1-10.0.0.5 redacts both ends while build2-10.0.0.5 stays put), and `ipv4-cidr` catches 'foo-10.0.0.0/8' via the trailing /0-32 CIDR signal. A range's head is caught by the base ipv4 detector and its tail by ipv4-range-tail — two independent spans mapped through the same correlation map, with the '-' left intact between them.

credit-card / ssn use a `validate` hook (Luhn+brand / SSN-assignment rules) so a broad regex stays precise; their fakes are deliberately INVALID (wrong Luhn check digit / never-assigned 000 area), which is what makes those detectors idempotent without a `safe` regex.

Phone is intentionally narrow — only E.164 (+ anchor) and NANP with separators and a [2-9] area/exchange — because broad phone patterns over-match numeric IDs in logs. GUIDs/UUIDs are deliberately NOT redacted: in logs they are correlation/component IDs, not personal identifiers.

detectors.name: ipv4
detectors.description: Dotted-quad IPv4 address (each octet 0-255). Not matched when preceded by '-' or '_' (likely part of a larger token) or by '~~' (a Windows assembly-identity / version marker, e.g. amd64~~10.0.1.2). A single '~' does NOT block the match (it can be a field separator). Fakes use RFC 5737 doc ranges, so `safe` never collides with a real IP.

detectors.name: ipv4-cidr
detectors.description: IPv4 address that is immediately followed by a /0-32 prefix — a high-confidence CIDR signal, so (unlike the base ipv4 detector) it has no '-' exclusion. The trailing /n is left intact (look-ahead).

detectors.name: ipv4-range-tail
detectors.description: The SECOND operand of a glued IPv4 range 'A.B.C.D-E.F.G.H'. The base ipv4 detector refuses any '-' on its left; this one re-allows it ONLY when the dash is itself preceded by a dotted-quad (a real range signal), so it rescues the range tail without matching identifier-glued addresses like 'build2-10.0.0.5'. Same category/`safe` as ipv4 (each endpoint is mapped independently and the '-' is preserved between them), so a redacted range scans clean and re-redacts idempotently.

detectors.name: ipv6
detectors.description: IPv6 address: full 8-group, '::'-compressed, IPv4-mapped (::ffff:1.2.3.4), and IPv4-embedded forms, with an optional %zone id. Requires 8 groups or a real '::' (bare '::' is NOT matched, to avoid C++ '::'/'std::' false positives); a preceding letter/digit also blocks the match. Fakes land in the RFC 3849 2001:db8::/32 doc range, shaped to mirror the original form.

detectors.name: credit-card
detectors.description: Payment card number, 13-19 digits with optional space/dash grouping. The `creditcard` validator (Luhn + brand prefix + length) suppresses the many numeric-ID false positives; only a number that actually checks out is redacted.

detectors.name: us-ssn
detectors.description: US Social Security Number in the dashed AAA-GG-SSSS form (the dashless 9-digit form is intentionally NOT matched — far too noisy in logs). The `ssn` validator rejects never-assigned area/group/serial values.

detectors.name: phone-e164
detectors.description: E.164 international phone number (leading +, 10-15 digits). The '+' anchor keeps this narrow.

detectors.name: phone-nanp
detectors.description: North American phone number with separators: '(NXX) NXX-XXXX' or 'NXX-NXX-XXXX', where the area and exchange start 2-9 (NANP rule). Fakes use the never-valid 000 area, so they are not re-matched (no `safe` needed).
